name: Amateur Lasagna
recipeIngredient: 60 ounces spaghetti sauce, two 30 oz. jars
2 cups ricotta cheese, part skim milk
1 1/2 cups mozzarella cheese, part skim milk, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the spaghetti sauce: Use about 1-1/2 to 2 jars of Ragu Sauce in a 30 ounce container.
2. Thaw the spinach and squeeze out any excess moisture.
3. In a large bowl, mix together the ricotta cheese, beaten egg, black pepper, oregano, basil, vegetable juice cocktail, and water. Stir until well combined.
4. Place a small amount of spaghetti sauce in the bottom of a 13 x 9 inch baking dish to prevent sticking and make removal easier later.
5. Layer one third of the uncooked lasagna noodles, one third of the sauce, and one third of the cheese-spinach mixture. Repeat these layers twice more, finishing with the sauce on top.
6. Carefully pour the water and vegetable juice cocktail (or tomato juice) around the edges of the casserole, gently moving the noodles to ensure even distribution of liquid.
7. Sprinkle grated parmesan cheese over the top if desired.
8. Cover tightly with foil and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour to 1 and 1/4 hours.
9. Remove the foil and continue baking for a few more minutes until lightly browned and bubbling.
10. Allow the lasagna to stand for at least 15 minutes before serving. Use a flat-edged spatula to carefully lift out portions for easy serving.
